Comparable sales refers to the percentage change in restaurant sales in one period from the same prior year period on a constant currency basis
System-wide sales growth refers to the percentage change in sales at all franchised restaurants and Company restaurants
Net restaurant growth refers to the net change in restaurant count (openings, net of permanent closures) over a trailing twelve-month period
the results exclude the effect of foreign currency translation (“FX Impact”)
our royalty revenues and advertising fund contributions are calculated based on a percentage of franchise sales

notes: Reviewed all 62 events in order plus 10 fiscal.ai KPIs. Most 8-Ks are item-list stubs with no narrative text; usable prose lives in the 10-K Business/Risk Factors, 10-Q MD&A definitions, and the 12 earnings-call transcripts.

KPI labels rewritten to lower_snake_case brand metrics; brand/segment names (Tim Hortons, Burger King, Popeyes, Firehouse Subs, International) are allowed as brands/segments, never the RBI legal name or QSR ticker.
Brand-level vs company-wide vs geo-brand metrics kept as separate drivers per ontology R9 (e.g. comparable_sales vs tim_hortons_canada_comparable_sales).
value_menu_demand (consumer preference shift, 10-K) kept separate from value_menu_promotions (company value-menu offering, transcript); reconcile may merge.
No eps_surprise/revenue_surprise coined: earnings 8-Ks are content-less stubs and no source text references consensus; operating_income_guidance is the only grounded guidance driver.

Skipped ~20 considered ideas as vague, instance-bound, or banned-word-only: ceo succession/executive-change 8-Ks (transition banned), debt-issuance and shareholder-vote 8-K stubs, Reg-FD stubs, Russia reporting exclusion, leap-day calendar effect, loyalty program (strategy boilerplate), franchisee sentiment (sentiment banned), adverse publicity (sentiment adjective), bare inflation/unemployment, leverage ratio, cold-beverage/protein-latte (folded into menu_innovation), espresso equipment, franchisee base count, coffee demand (coffee_price kept), corporate cost savings/reorganization, restaurant_count_guidance and capex_guidance (covered by base drivers).
